title: New: Guided Roll walkthrough on the Overload Rolling tool
notes: New "Guided Roll" tab on the Overload Rolling tool: enter one gear piece's current lines and the stats + tiers you want, and it prints a tight, step-by-step roll plan for that exact piece.

The plan splits into two phases — first reroll for the right stats (locking each as it lands), then value-reset every line up to its target tier — and tells you which lines to lock, which to leave alone, and when.

It follows the game's real roll odds: a rare line (Line 2 appears in 50% of rolls, Line 3 in 30%) gets locked the moment it lands a stat you need — take whichever comes first — while the always-present Line 1 is left rerolling so you never waste a lock on it.

title: Shotgun accuracy by range
notes: Shotgun hit and core-hit rates at each range are now grounded in the game's actual on-screen aim geometry — we measured the spread circle, crosshair, and boss core frame-by-frame and mapped the game's accuracy-circle values to their real sizes.

This lets a shotgun's pellet landing and core rate at close, mid, and far range come from measured geometry instead of a hand-picked estimate, with run-to-run variance handled by the existing seeded simulation.
Used data from minas. and _TricK_ to calculate shotgun pellet count and core hit rate.

title: More nikkes modeled
notes: We've increased the number of modeled nikkes, so more units now simulate with their complete kits.

title: New: Max Ammo breakpoints + clearer Overload tools + DPS profiles
notes: The Charge Speed Breakpoints tool is now Overload Breakpoints, holding two calculators in one place — Charge Speed and a new Max Ammo one.
New Max Ammo calculator: pick any nikke and see every extra round she can reach and how many overload lines each one costs (defaults to Maiden: Ice Rose).
Both breakpoint tables now lead with an OL Lines column, and you can choose which overload tier (T1–T15, default T11) to measure against — the picker shows that tier's actual roll value.
On the Overload Rolling tool, the per-line tier dropdowns now show the real roll value for the stat you picked (e.g. T11 ATK = 68.93%) instead of a bare "T11."
Optimize Overload now lets you choose which overload tier (T1–T15, default T11) the line ranking is measured at — the whole 8/12 set and the four free lines all scale to that tier.
Optimize Overload's control team now defaults to Solo instead of Standard, so you see a nikke's own overload gains without a support team's buffs baked in.
The "Calculate chance to roll" hand-off from Optimize Overload now carries your chosen tier into the Overload Rolling tool instead of always sending T11.
Bready now has a Sustained / Distributed taste picker on her card — matching how her kit switches taste based on the buff type her team feeds her (defaults to Sustained).

The DPS Rankings page has a new "Custom profiles" section that spells out the modeling choices behind the chart: Bready is run in her Distributed taste, Diesel: Winter Sweets is assumed to burst second (her higher-damage Highlight state, not yet reflected in her score), Red Hood and Rapi: Red Hood operate as B3, and every favorite-item unit is assumed to have its favorite item fully unlocked.

title: DPS Rankings: filter by element
notes: New element buttons (All / Fire / Water / Wind / Electric / Iron) under the compare-a-unit picker — pick one and every chart on the page shows only that element's B3 carries.
An element view ranks every B3 of that color, not just the top-tier headliners — so you can see where the whole element stands, down through the lower tiers.
While an element is selected, the compare-a-unit readout ranks within that element, and copied links and share images carry the filter with them.

title: Accuracy: Rapi: Red Hood, Soda, Cinderella: Crystal Wave
notes: Rapi: Red Hood — her attached-rocket explosions now land the way the game places them: a core hit about a third of the time, detonating on contact during Full Burst, and critting like her other shots. Her damage reads much closer to real fights.
Soda: Twinkling Bunny — fixed how her Golden Chips drive her crit damage (the stack starts full, not from zero) and confirmed her burst ATK buff fires every burst, not just the first.
Cinderella: Crystal Wave — her Full Burst core-strike hit now correctly registers as a core hit inside Full Burst, closing a ~13% under-count.

title: New: Roster Sim + sharper portraits
notes: New Roster Sim tab (right next to Team Sim): enter up to five teams of five and sim them all at once under one shared loadout + boss profile. You get each team's damage, a damage bar per team, and the roster total — with each nikke usable only once across the whole roster, matching the solo-raid rule.
The Solo-Raid Roster Generator now has a "Copy to Roster Sim" button, so you can take its five teams, tweak them, and re-sim.
Logged in? You can now save and load whole rosters, not just single teams.
Sharper unit portraits across the team and roster views (and the pick-a-slot grid), and the roster cards now center neatly when the last row isn't full. The old "Sim" tab is now called "Team Sim".

title: UI refresh + share images now use unit portraits
notes: Mobile refresh: the page now uses the full screen width, the boss and "apply to all" filters wrap into even rows instead of lopsided ones, and the navigation collapses into a compact "current tab + dropdown" so long tab names no longer run off the edge.
The Optimal Team and Solo-Raid Roster generators now show an at-a-glance row of unit portraits; the roster lays its five teams out as a centered grid plus compact per-team cards.
Copy-image share cards now render real unit portraits instead of placeholder boxes — including a new roster share card that shows all five teams with total-damage bars.

title: Damage model: range-aware core hits + shotgun overhaul
notes: Auto-aim core-hit rate is now range-dependent. The sim used to assume one flat core-hit chance per weapon; it now models that auto-aim finds the boss core far more often up close and rarely at range — measured from recorded fights. Assault Rifles / SMGs / Shotguns lose core damage when the boss is far; Machine Guns, Snipers and Rocket Launchers are largely unchanged.

Shotguns fully re-modeled from footage — pellet landing and core-hit rate are now measured per range band against in-fight damage totals (earlier popup-counting under-read the dense clusters of overlapping pellet numbers). Two shotgun units now match their real recordings within about 1%, and the whole shotgun roster reads far truer.
Little Mermaid and other SMG/AR carries grade noticeably more accurately now that their core hits aren't over-credited at range.
Newly modeled kits: Soda: Twinkling Bunny's Golden Chip stacking buffs, and Dorothy: Serendipity's single-pellet consolidation mode (her big consolidated shots now land at full value).
Roster cleanup: removed 11 off-meta units that aren't part of the tracked top-ranker teams.

title: New: Doll Leveling tool
notes: A new Doll Leveling tool finds the cheapest way to level your dolls (Collection Items) to SR phase 15 — it shows exactly which kit to feed at each phase, and how many SR dolls you can expect to finish per 1000 kit-boxes.
The big tip: use all your kits — don't hoard. Blue kits are your workhorse, and Gold kits are best saved for the phase 10-to-15 push. Played right, that's about 77 SR dolls per 1000 boxes.

"Level from Current" tells you what to feed next from your doll's current phase, and a plain-English FAQ covers the overall strategy and whether to trade or launder spare R dolls (short answer: trade them).

title: Tools menu + Overload Rolling upgrades
notes: The calculators now live under a new Tools menu: Overload Rolling, Doll Leveling, Charge Speed Breakpoints, Optimal Team Generator, and Solo-Raid Roster Generator.
Overload Rolling (formerly Overload Roll Sim) now shows the common 8/12 build cost the instant you open it — no setup needed.
The Overload results table now shows a separate "95% of the time" figure for both rerolls and module cost, and the per-piece numbers add up cleanly to the full-build total.

title: New: Overload Roll Sim
notes: A new Overload Roll Sim page estimates how many rerolls and Custom Modules it takes to hit a target Overload build — set your target lines and minimum tiers per piece and see the expected cost, per-piece and total, with a full distribution curve.
"Roll from Current" lets you enter the lines you already have and see just the remaining cost to reach your goal.

It models the real two-step method — reroll for the right stats, then value-reset each line up to tier — using the most efficient locking strategy (for example, it won't waste a lock on a weak Line 1 while you're still chasing a 12/12).

A new FAQ answers the common questions in plain English: the best way to roll an 8/12 vs a 12/12, whether to lock a T15 black line, the odds of rolling T11 or higher, and the odds of hitting all three lines at once.
On the Optimize Overload page, a new "Calculate chance to roll" button sends the best damage lines straight into the sim so you can see what they'll cost to actually roll.

title: Overload optimizer, charge-speed breakpoints & UI refresh
notes: Refreshed the site's look and navigation — and the whole app now works in a mobile / portrait layout.
New Optimize Overload page ranks how a carry should spend its four overload lines by real in-team damage, and it's weapon-aware, so it only suggests lines that actually help that unit.
New Charge Speed Breakpoints page shows, per unit, exactly how much charge speed you need to shave each frame off a charge weapon — and where extra charge speed is wasted.

title: New: DPS Chart
notes: A new DPS Chart page ranks the top Burst-3 carries head-to-head under standardized support teams — choose the support framework, elemental advantage, core-hit rate, and investment level to compare.
Three at-a-glance boards up top (Standard Scope Lock, Hyper Carry 8/12, and Anis Hyper Carry 8/12), each shown at 0 / 50 / 100% core.
Pick any Burst-3 unit to see exactly where it lands on every chart — including units outside the top 10.
Copy a link or an image of any chart to share it.
The DPS Test tab can now output these same infographics and toggle between your own custom control group and the standardized matrix.

title: Launch — the measured-mechanics sim
notes: Elemental advantage was reworked into its own damage bucket, and the machine-gun range bonus now applies only in the far distance band — both matching frame-counted recordings.

Snow White: Heavy Arms and Rapi: Red Hood were re-modeled onto their measured in-fight behavior, the Mint + Prika duet burst order was fixed, and Liberalio's bonus-damage procs now correctly receive the Full Burst bonus when they land inside a Full Burst window.
Fifteen recorded fights now match the sim's full-burst rotation exactly, frame-graded from video.
The accuracy board sits at a ~0.95 median across 86 unit readings, with 63% of readings within ±10% of the real fight.
New site pages: the DPS Test tab (scope-locked control team vs. variable-group comparison), the Mechanics reference page, and this Dev page with patch notes.
